zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean d'Harcourt III於大約1276年出生在法國諾門迪艾哈爾庫特時，他的父親Jean d'HARCOURT II,  le Preux37歲，母親Jeanne de CHATELLERAULT38歲。他於1302年與Alix de Brabant結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1355年1月8日在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利盧享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: d'Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jean d'Harcourt III was born about 1276, in Harcourt, Eure, Normandy, France, his father, Jean d'HARCOURT II,  le Preux, was 37 and his mother, Jeanne de CHATELLERAULT, was 38. He married Alix de Brabant in 1302. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 8 January 1355, in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, at the age of 80.

Il a épousé Alix de Brabant en 1302. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 8 janvier 1355, à Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 80 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Jean d'Harcourt III(이)가 대략 1276년 프랑스 노먼디 우르 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean d'HARCOURT II,  le Preux(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de CHATELLERAULT(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1302년 Alix de Brabant(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1355년 1월 8일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 루언에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Alix de Brabant em 1302. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de janeiro de 1355, em Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean d'Harcourt III родился около 1276 г. в местности харкоурт, эур, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean d'HARCOURT II,  le Preux было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de CHATELLERAULT, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Alix de Brabant в 1302 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 8 января 1355 г. в местности роуен, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция в возрасте 80 лет.

Se casó con Alix de Brabant en 1302. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 8 de enero de 1355, en Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 80 años.
